transcript: Hi, I was lookin’ for that single. Think it’s by Flip Child
Hm, I’m sorry, I’m not familiar with that. Um, we carry, mostly, older records; we don’t have a lot of new releases.
Yeah, it goes, like, “you talkin’ ‘bout cha flip wile, you buckwile; talkin’ ‘bout cha buckchile, you buckchile. Talkin’ ‘bout cha”.
Sorry, I don’t think I’ve ever heard that song before.
And it’s like, “talkin’ ‘bout cha flip wile, you flip chile; talkin’ ‘bout cha stepchile, he buckwile”. And it’s like, “talkin’ ‘bout cha flip”.
Sorry, I don’t—I don’t know it. Um, thanks for checkin’, though.
“Talkin’ ‘bout cha flip”. Where do you recommend for somethin’ like this?
Alright, thanks for the call, but I think I’m gonna hang up now.
‘Cause I don’t have what you’re lookin’ for. I don’t know who the artist is; I don’t know anything about it.
How long you been workin’? You new, or…?
It’s all over the radio. So I guess you just missed it, or…?
And It’s like—like, “talkin’ like you buckwile, you buckiwile—“
—“talkin’ ‘bout ya flipwile, you flipchile”. Like, “talkin’ like you buckwile, you buckwile”—
—Yeah, I got the lyrics, but who—
“talkin’ ‘bout ya flipwile, you flipchile”. Like, “talkin’ like ya buckwile”—
—Alright dude, I’m not gonna be able to help you.
And it’s like—it’s like, “talkin’ like you buckwile—“
Dude, I already—I—you’ve sang it already. I—I don’t—
“you buckchile; talkin’ ‘bout ya buckchile, he buckwile”.
Okay, dude. Have a good one.
I have a credit card; we could do a special order by a credit card. [exhales]
—Yeah, have a great New Year’s dude. Peace!—
—I can’t see anything listed for a “Doctor Sheba”—
[pitch-up] “talkin’ ‘bout ya buckwile, you buckwile; talkin’ ‘bout ya flipchile, he buckwile” [sfx] “talkin’ ‘bout ya”.
Uh. [laughs] Am I on, like, a prank show or Crank Yankers or something?
And I’m at a payphone. I’ve been trying to ride the bus up there to buy this record. I can give ya a credit card to reserve it.
I’ve said that I’ve, ah, never heard that before, in my life. And I don’t have whatever it is you’re lookin’ for.
Do you have a keyboard and samplers at the payphone, too?
Here, lemme give you the 12-inch person.
Hi! I was tryin’ to get a single that I was hoping to buy.
Uh, Dr. Sheba; I’m pretty sure they’re Babylonian.
Oh, no. I don’t have that.
—Nah, we don’t really got new—new stuff like that, no—
—“you buckwile, you buckwile; talkin’ like you flipchile, you flipwile—“
—Yeah, I—I hear ya, but no, we don’t have that.—
—Yo, stop singing the song at me. I don’t have it.—
—“talkin’ like you buckwile, you buckchile”. It’s like—like, “talkin’ like you flipwile”
Actually, you spoke to me a little while ago and I wasn’t having any luck finding this single from you. I know you’re telling me they’re from Calcutta?
Babylonian. Okay. Um, unfortunately, I haven’t been able to find anything about this band at all. Um—
“Talkin’ like you buckwile, you buckwile; talkin’ like ya flipchile, he flipwile”. You feel me?
Right, right, right. You—you—you had sung it before over the phone to me and, um, I wasn’t able to—
—“Talkin’ ‘bout ya flipchile, he flipwile; talkin’ like ya”—‘Kay, what do you need from me, a credit card, or…?
No. I would actually need to be able to find the—the record so I could order it. Yeah, I wouldn’t take anybody’s—
—It’s on DodecaQuintron; hot imprint outta Calcutta.
I don’t see any “Deck of Quintron”, I don’t see any “Flipchild” or “Doctor Sheba”.
“Talkin’ like ya buckwile, you buckwile; talkin’ like ya flipchile—“
—Right. I’m sorry; the only time I’ve heard this song is when you sang it earlier and now—
—“he stepchile; talkin’ like ya buckwile, you buckwile; talkin’ like ya f—“
Uh, I’m gonna go back to work.
Ya feel me on the lyrics?
You sing like a piano player.
Well no. That’s just how it goes.—
—No, no. I’m just saying your voice. That—
—“Talkin’ like ya buckwile, he buckchile; talkin’ like ya flipwile, you flipchile; talkin’ like you buckwile—“
But it’s like, “talkin’ like you flipchile—“
—I got it, I got it. I understand. [stammers] prank call of somethin’?
—“you flipwile; talkin’ like ya buckwild, you—“
I wish I was recording you doing that that!
I’m more enjoying you just doing it, actually. Um, it’s—so it’s more like hip-hop, right?
—“talkin’ like you buckwile, you buckwile; talkin’ like you f—“
No, I’m at the bus stop at the payphone; I’m gonna ride the bus up ‘ere, so—
—Yo, this has to be Longmont Potion Castle, right?—
—talkin’ like you flipchile”. What’re you getting at, here?
Do you know who Longmont Potion Castle is?
—and he does this stuff where he’ll, like, delay his vocals and stuff like that.

No, no, no, no! He he’s a—he’s a comedian. Anyway, it just sounds like—I—I couldn’t tell if you pranking me or not, ‘cause I heard, like, delay in your vocals and stuff. So, its called, “Doctor Sheba Buckwild”?
Flipchild? Oh, my gosh! I gotta hear this shit, you know? “Flip. Child”. I don’t think we do have that.
I did. “Somethin’ like a flipchild, flipwild”? What was it?
He goes, “talkin’ like ya buckwile, you buckwile; talkin’ like ya flipwile, you flipwile; talkin’ like ya buckwile, ya buckwile; talkin’ like ya flip—“
—Yo, it is Longmont! Yo, hey, I am a big fan. This has to be Longmont, right?
It has to be. Hey, you’re the man! I’ve been listening to you for a while! [laughs] The Alex Trebek stuff, especially, I was really into—
—“talkin’ like ya buckwile!—“ Dude, what a pleasure, man!
Can I help you with something?
Hi! Yeah, I just wanted the scoop; the single, not the full-length.
What wa—[sighs] I’m s—I’m sorry, what were you lookin’ for?
It goes—goes, “talkin’ like you buckwild, you buckwile; talkin’ like you—“
—Yeah, we—we don’t know what that is, man—
—We don’t know what that is. You’re was—
—You’re wasting your time, you’re wasting our time. So, you’re gonna have to go somewhere else. I’m sorry—
—It’s by Dr. Sheba. “Talkin’ like you buck—“. You know?
You called us already. We’ve answered you multiple times. You’re wastin’ my time, so—
—I got a credit card. Special order it for me—
—We’re not interested! We’re not interested in your money! Go somewhere else!
I was lookin’ for that “Flipchile” record. The 45?—
—Hey—hey, why don’t you fuck off and stop calling—
—It goes, “talkin’ bout ya flipchild, you buckwile”
—Alright, fuck off, you piece of shit!—
“Doctor Sheba”. “Flipchild”. I couldn’t find anything on it when I try to find out—
That’s the name of the song they make. Did he sing to you already?
Yeah, he’ll sing it to you again.
It’s Dr. Sheba, is the artist? Yeah—
—I just wanted the scoop, the single.
Yeah, they don’t have anything by Dr. Sheba.
—Yeah, I heard you. I—you—you already s—
—“You talkin’ like you’re stepchild, you stepwile—“
—You already sang it to me; I don’t need to hear it again—
—I don’t know if I’m ringin’ a bell or not.
I’ve never heard it in my life, so no sense in doin’ that—
—It’s like, “talkin’ like you’re buckwild, you buckwile—“
—Yeah, there’s really—there’s really no sense in singing it to me—
—‘cause I’ve never heard it in my life.
So, yeah. It’s Dr. Sheba. We can’t get it for you; our distributor doesn’t carry it, so we can’t get it for you.
—[speaking to employee in background] He won’t fucking stop!—
